sections.reason: The real-hardware hypothesis is refuted and the analysis identifies concrete, addressable transfer gaps: (1) launch-overhead/small-config latency is unmodeled, (2) efficiency factors are misspecified for real B200 scheduling, and (3) bottleneck labels lack hardware-counter ground truth. The best next step is a targeted experiment that adds a launch-overhead term, expands the calibration search space, and revalidates on real B200 measurements.

summary: Route to a targeted follow-up experiment to close the real-hardware transfer gaps.
title: Analysis Route Decision: Follow-up Experiment
